input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image showing seven CAD views (each boxed in black) of our vehicle design. The top, big view shows a close-up view of the design. The six smaller views on the bottom of the image show different complete views of the CAD of the vehicle and are provided for context. Note that the close-up view orientation matches one of the six complete view orientations. The close-up view may also have some components hidden (with respect to the corresponding complete view) for visualization of specific components. Looking at the close-up view, is/are any part of the the brakes or the brake system or the brake visible in the close-up view? Answer simply with yes or no.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.3.4.1
            TITLE: the vehicle must have a brake light that is clearly visible from the rear in very bright sunlight.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.4.1: The vehicle must have a Brake Light that is clearly visible from the rear in very bright sunlight.
T.3.4.1 The vehicle must have a Brake Light that is clearly visible from the rear in very bright sunlight.

RULE: in.12.1
            TITLE: objective

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.12.1: Objective
IN.12.1 Objective 
The brake system will be dynamically tested and must demonstrate the capability of locking all 
four wheels when stopping the vehicle in a straight line at the end of an acceleration run 
specified by the brake inspectors

RULE: v.3.1.3
            TITLE: all suspension mounting points must be visible at technical inspection by direct view or by

            CONTENT:
            rule V.3.1.3: All suspension mounting points must be visible at Technical Inspection by direct view or by
V.3.1.3 All suspension mounting points must be visible at Technical Inspection by direct view or by 
removing any covers.

RULE: ic.5.4.6
            TITLE: the sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank,

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.5.4.6: The sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank,
IC.5.4.6 The sight tube and fuel level line must be clearly visible to two individuals (one to fill the tank, 
the other to visually verify fill) without the need of assistance (artificial lighting, magnifiers, 
etc) or the need to remove any parts (body panels, etc).

RULE: in.2.3
            TITLE: visible access

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.2.3: Visible Access
IN.2.3 Visible Access  
All items on the Technical Inspection Form must be clearly visible to the technical inspectors 
without using instruments such as endoscopes or mirrors.  
Methods to provide visible access include but are not limited to removable body panels, access 
panels, and other components
